title: File-Push Kiss
setup: Your queen on **e7** and king on **f6** form a tight pair — but {{e8}} would let the king run.
hint: Push the queen *up the file* of the king — your king defends her landing square.
explanation:
## The Idea

Another **kiss-of-death**. Going to {{e8}} lets the king walk away because **f6** doesn't defend e8 — but the queen one square in front of the king, defended by your king, is the killer.

Play {{Qf7#}}.

Why is the king stuck?

- The queen on f7 attacks f8 directly (file check)
- {{e8}} — covered by the queen on f7 (diagonal)
- {{g8}} — covered by the queen on f7 (diagonal)
- {{Kxf7}} is illegal — your king on **f6** defends f7
- Nothing can interpose: f7 is adjacent to f8

> **Takeaway:** Beware of *flashy queen checks from afar* — they often allow escape. The mating queen often lands *next to* the enemy king on a square defended by your king.
